---
# Copyright © 2023-2026 ValidMind Inc. All rights reserved.
# Refer to the LICENSE file in the root of this repository for details.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0 AND ValidMind Commercial
title: "Autogenerate validation reports"
date: last-modified
---

Streamline your validation workflow with AI-powered evidence linking and risk assessment generation. These features help validators efficiently link relevant evidence to guidelines and generate risk assessments across your validation reports.

::: {.attn}

## Prerequisites

- [x] {{< var link.login >}}
- [x] The model you are validating is registered in the model inventory.[^1]
- [x] A model developer has submitted their model documentation for validation.[^2]
- [x] You are a [{{< fa circle-check >}} Validator]{.bubble} or assigned another role with sufficient permissions.[^3]

:::

## Auto-link evidence

Auto-Link Evidence uses AI to suggest relevant evidence blocks for each validation guideline, reducing the manual effort of finding and linking supporting documentation.

### Link evidence to a single guideline

1. In the left sidebar, click **{{< fa cubes >}} Inventory**.

2. Select the model you are validating.[^4]

3. In the left sidebar, click **{{< fa file >}} Documents** and select the **Latest** tab.

4. Click on a validation report.

5. Navigate to the validation guideline you want to link evidence to.

6. Click **{{< fa wand-magic-sparkles >}} Auto-Link Evidence**.

7. Select the evidence type to map:
   - **Developer Evidence** — Evidence logged by developers via the {{< var validmind.developer >}}
   - **Validator Evidence** — Evidence uploaded or created by validators

8. Review the suggested evidence blocks. Each suggestion shows:
   - Block name and type (test result, text block, or metadata)
   - Relevance score indicating how well the evidence matches the guideline
   - Explanation of why the evidence was suggested
   - Preview of the evidence content

9. Select the evidence blocks you want to link:
   - Click **Select all** to link all suggested evidence
   - Individually select or deselect evidence blocks as needed

10. Click **Assign Evidence** to persist the links.

The linked evidence appears in the **Manage Evidence** dialog for that guideline, organized by evidence type.

### Link evidence to all guidelines

To run auto-link across all guidelines at once:

1. From the validation report, click **{{< fa layer-group >}} Evidence Mapper** to open the bulk view.

2. Click **{{< fa wand-magic-sparkles >}} Auto-Link Evidence**.

3. Select the evidence type to map.

4. The system processes all guidelines and shows a progress indicator while mapping runs.

5. Review suggested evidence for each guideline:
   - Navigate between guidelines to see suggestions
   - Select or deselect evidence blocks per guideline

6. Click **Assign Evidence** to persist all links at once.

## Generate risk assessments

Risk Assessment Generation uses AI to automatically populate risk assessment content based on linked evidence, saving validators time while maintaining quality.

### Generate a risk assessment for a single guideline

1. Navigate to the validation guideline you want to assess.

2. Click **{{< fa wand-magic-sparkles >}} Generate Risk Assessment**.

3. The system analyzes linked evidence and generates risk assessment content including:

   - **Guideline Assessment** — Compliance conclusion, evidence adequacy, and compliance strength
   - **Validation Observations** — Discrete findings ordered by severity
   - **Evidence Review** — Technical support for the assessment

4. Review the generated content in the risk assessment editor.

5. Edit the generated content as needed — changes are auto-saved.

### Generate risk assessments for all guidelines

To generate risk assessments across all guidelines at once:

1. From the validation report, click **{{< fa layer-group >}} Evidence Mapper** to open the bulk view.

2. Click **{{< fa wand-magic-sparkles >}} Generate All Risk Assessments**.

3. The system processes all unlocked guidelines and shows streaming progress as results come in.

::: {.callout title="Locked guidelines are preserved"}
Bulk risk assessment generation respects per-guideline locks. Guidelines you have manually locked are skipped to preserve curated content.
:::

4. Review the generated assessments for each guideline.

5. Make any adjustments needed — all changes are auto-saved.

## Understanding AI-generated assessments

Each AI-generated risk assessment follows a structured format:

### Guideline Assessment

Three paragraphs covering:

1. **Compliance Conclusion** — Whether the guideline is met, partially met, or not met
2. **Evidence Adequacy** — Assessment of documentation completeness
3. **Compliance Strength** — Evaluation of model condition and performance

### Validation Observations

A list of discrete findings or strengths, each:

- Covering a single issue or strength
- Including developer-facing actions where applicable
- Ordered by severity

### Evidence Review

Detailed technical support including:

- Summary of developer-provided evidence
- Summary of independent validation testing (if available)
- Specific metrics, thresholds, and results

## What's next

- [Assess compliance](assess-compliance.qmd)
- [Working with artifacts](working-with-artifacts.qmd)


<!-- FOOTNOTES -->

[^1]: [Register records in the inventory](/guide/inventory/register-records-in-inventory.qmd)

[^2]: [Submit for approval](/guide/model-documentation/submit-for-approval.qmd)

[^3]: [Manage permissions](/guide/configuration/manage-permissions.qmd)

[^4]: [Working with the inventory](/guide/inventory/working-with-the-inventory.qmd#search-filter-and-sort-records)
